overview: The story of the controversial and influential Black Nationalist leader, from his early life and career as a small-time gangster to his ministry as a member of the Nation of Islam.
title: Malcolm X

overview: Following the Battle of Antietam, Colonel Robert Gould Shaw leads the 54th Massachusetts Volunteer Infantry, the Union Army's first all-Black regiment, during the American Civil War.
title: Glory

overview: An epic tale of a young woman's struggle to find her identity in the rural American South, surviving abuse and separation across forty years.
title: The Color Purple

overview: On the hottest day of the year in a Brooklyn neighborhood, racial tensions between residents boil over into violence and tragedy.
title: Do the Right Thing

overview: The true story of Jackie Robinson, who broke Major League Baseball's color barrier in 1947 when he was signed by the Brooklyn Dodgers.
The untold story of the Black women mathematicians at NASA whose calculations helped launch astronaut John Glenn into orbit during the Space Race.
title: Hidden Figures

overview: Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. leads a dangerous campaign to secure equal voting rights in the face of violent opposition with the epic march from Selma to Montgomery in 1965.
title: Selma

overview: A high school football coach in Virginia integrates his team in 1971, uniting Black and white players who learn to overcome their differences and win.
title: Remember the Titans

overview: Chronicles the dramatic story of an idealistic young man's journey through life and Black music, from gospel roots in the South to the heights of an R&B vocal group.
title: The Five Heartbeats

overview: Three generations of barbers serve a Chicago South Side neighborhood, where the shop becomes a haven for community, debate, and laughter over the course of one eventful day.
title: Barbershop

overview: In 1960s Detroit, a talented trio of singers from the projects rises to stardom and faces the personal costs of fame in the booming Motown era.
title: Dreamgirls

overview: A janitor at MIT possesses a genius-level intellect but must confront his troubled past with the help of a therapist before he can move forward with his life.
title: Good Will Hunting

overview: Two imprisoned men bond over a number of years, finding solace and eventual redemption through acts of common decency at Shawshank State Penitentiary.
title: The Shawshank Redemption

overview: The aging patriarch of an organized crime dynasty transfers control of his clandestine empire to his reluctant youngest son in postwar New York.
title: The Godfather

overview: An undercover cop and a mole in the police attempt to identify each other while infiltrating an Irish gang in South Boston.
title: The Departed

overview: The story of middleweight boxer Rubin 'Hurricane' Carter, wrongly convicted of a triple murder, and the decades-long fight to overturn his conviction and win his freedom.
title: The Hurricane

overview: A small-time boxer from Philadelphia gets a once-in-a-lifetime shot at the heavyweight championship, determined to go the distance and prove his worth.
title: Rocky

overview: A wealthy white couple takes in a homeless Black teenager and helps him become an All-American football player and first-round NFL draft pick.
title: The Blind Side

overview: The biography of legendary R&B and soul musician Ray Charles, tracing his rise from poverty in the South to international stardom despite blindness and addiction.
title: Ray

overview: A faith-driven drama in which a struggling football coach inspires his team and community to overcome adversity through perseverance and belief.
title: Facing the Giants

overview: A firefighter struggling to save his failing marriage embarks on a 40-day experiment to demonstrate love, rooted in his renewed faith.
title: Fireproof

overview: An elderly Jewish widow in the American South forms an unlikely decades-long friendship with her dignified Black chauffeur amid changing times.
title: Driving Miss Daisy

overview: A documentary exploring the racial inequities within the United States criminal justice system and the legacy of slavery through the lens of the prison population.
title: 13th

overview: Set in Depression-era Texas, an underdog debate team from a small Black college takes on the nation's best, challenging segregation through the power of words.
title: The Great Debaters
